mnemonics: PRIORITY Lines: 'High Number Has High Priority' - Reminds that in a priority encoder, the highest active input line (largest number) gets priority and its code is output.
ENCODE Inputs: 'Eight Needs Three' - An 8-to-3 encoder has 8 inputs and 3 outputs, following the rule that 2^n inputs need n outputs.
keyPoints: An encoder converts multiple active inputs (like a keypress) into a coded binary output.
A standard encoder assumes only one input is active at a time; if multiple are active, the output can be undefined.
A priority encoder resolves multiple active inputs by outputting the code for the highest-priority input.
commonMistakes: Assuming a standard encoder will work correctly with multiple active inputs; it requires a priority encoder.
Forgetting that the number of output lines (n) for an encoder is determined by the number of inputs (m) using the formula m ≤ 2^n.
